text: Deserts are inhospitable during Summer, and supply lines can not be established through them.
Alpine passes are blocked by snow during Winter, and supply lines can not be established through them.

War dictates that unnatural allies fight together and that drastic measures are taken to defeat a common enemy. However, the Greek world does not look kindly upon expansionism.\\n\\nThe capture of the capital cities – Pella, Pergamon, Rodos and Athens, will cause [[col:red]]severe diplomatic penalties.[[/col]] Instead, aim to erode enemy influence by striking at their smaller possessions. Recapturing your own capital will not incur a penalty.

Once a Carthaginian ally and within their sphere of influence, Messina has been taken within the Roman area of control. This new push into Sicily has caused the two major powers to begin eyeing the island between them as the staging ground for war. Although they were once allies, the two preeminent powers of the Western Mediterrenean now seem on a collision course for conflict. The aging hegemony of the Carthaginian Republic now faces an up and coming Rome that could threaten its power in the region.

After years of enmity have built up between the Hellenic overlords of the Seleukid dynasty and their eastern vassals, the desire for independence has boiled over. Multiple satrapies have rebelled against their overlords, hoping to carve out their own kingdoms and call upon the ancient glories of Persia's past.

Although the great Seleukid empire stretches across the known Persian world, the hold over these lands is tenuous at best. Alexander was a force of personality that held together a disparate empire of multiple cultures and religions. Will you be able to do the same? Should your eastern satrapies begin to look upon their overlords with suspicion and disdain, the possibility of revolt will grow ever higher. Be careful to watch their diplomatic levels and prepare for the worst should too many of them grow in power and decide you are no longer a necessity for rule.

We are relative newcomers in this land, but we mean to make our mark. Our culture and heritage will spread across this land and well into the northern steppes. As we gain more knowledge from these nomadic lands and spread our traditions there, we may see some adaptations of our own weapons and tactics.

We have reached a new military reform.\\n\\nAs our lands and culture have expanded into the nomadic steppes, new ways of warfare have been developed. Learning from an enemy can be as valuable a tool in warfare as a sword or shield.

Maurya has reached a new military reform.\\n\\nAs their lands and culture have expanded into the nomadic steppes, new ways of warfare have been developed. Learning from an enemy can be as valuable a tool in warfare as a sword or shield.

Trait: Brawler\\n\\nAfter a battle in which his presence only served to get his bodyguards killed and endanger those around him, this man is no longer viewed as great a physical thread on the battlefield as he used to be. Perhaps in time and with more personal moments of glory in battles he may regain his previous stature as a more feared combatant.

Trait: Commander\\n\\nAlthough he may be successful at times on the field of battle, this man's strategies have lost too many of his men to injury and death. Perhaps he is not as brilliant a tactician as he believed himself to be. Given time and more careful planning, he may yet regain his confidence and the positive perception of those around him as a competent commander.

Trait: Intellectual\\n\\nThis man has failed to govern the local province properly and the public unrest in the region reflects that lack of judgement. Perhaps with further study at libraries and academies, along with improved results, this man will regain his status as a trusted intellectual figure.

After many battles lost, this general has seen the horrors of war up close. Comrades lying dead on the battlefield, enemy swords at his throat, and the incessant cries of the wounded have blunted this man's bravery. After this latest battle where his closest friends have been slaughtered, this man now sees the world in a much more jaded light.

After many battles lost, this general has seen the horrors of war up close. Comrades lying dead on the battlefield, enemy swords at his throat, and the incessant cries of the wounded have blunted this man's bravery. He has gone from being full of himself to obsessively worried.

After many battles lost, this general has seen the horrors of war up close. Comrades lying dead on the battlefield, enemy swords at his throat, and the incessant cries of the wounded have blunted this man's bravery. Perhaps his nature is better suited to the pen than the sword.

Once he shied away from battle, but now this man has learned to steel himself against the oncoming tides of war. His character has been hardened and shown true by the rigors of war. Of course, that also leads to an extra helping of self confidence.

Once he shied away from battle, but now this man has learned to steel himself against the oncoming tides of war. His character has been hardened and shown true by the rigors of war. He has turned his love of knowledge and virtue into a strong personality trait.

Once he shied away from battle, but now this man has learned to steel himself against the oncoming tides of war. His character has been hardened and shown true by the rigors of war. His anxious nature has been supressed by a new found confidence.

Once he shied away from battle, but now this man has learned to steel himself against the oncoming tides of war. His character has been hardened and shown true by the rigors of war. After his latest defensive victory, this man sees the world as something to be embraced and controlled.

Once he shied away from battle, but now this man has learned to steel himself against the oncoming tides of war. His character has been hardened and shown true by the rigors of war. From being doubtful of the world, this man has learned to now embrace it.

Rather than rushing headlong into combat in the thick of battle, this man has learned to temper his rash nature with prudence. The latest battle with so many dead and wounded has tempered his previously unhinged nature. He is still courageous, but he is also smart.

The constant roar of exultation from his troops, along with easy victories, has pushed this man over the edge. He knows no limits and tolerates no impediment. To glory!

Overzealous whim has crushed this man's once forceful soul. The repetition of defeat and the loss of his men has sent him into a downward spiral. Constant apparitions of dead friends and soldiers haunt his waking dreams.

Rather than rushing headlong into combat in the thick of battle, this man has learned to temper his rash nature with prudence. The latest battle with so many dead and wounded has tempered his previously unhinged nature. He is still courageous, but he is also smart.

After his great victory against the enemies of Roma, this man has earned the acclamation of the people and his peers in the Senate. He should travel back to Roma in order to celebrate his triumph. He will ride through the streets, a god for a day! With the red face of Jupiter and the procession of his spoils, time will never forget this man's glorious contributions to the Republic.

Riding through the streets of Roma, this man has experienced a reward that few will ever know. The people scream his name, his ancestors are venerated, and the Senate recognizes his pre-eminent glory. From this day's celebration forward, he shall be known as vir triumphalis, scion of the Republic.
New innovations in military technology and strategy have spread through various Greek, Thracian, Illyrian, Galatian and other cultures to impact the style and equipment of those factions.
New innovations in military technology and strategy have spread through various Greek, Thracian, Illyrian, Galatian and other cultures to impact the style and equipment of those factions.
One of the local chieftans has seen the fortitude and leadership of this man and recommended him for more responsibility.
Those in power have seen enough of one this man's quality to know he must be promoted.
The ruling oligarchs have seen potential in this man. He has been promoted toward higher responsibility.
This man has been elected quaestor. The lowest position on the cursus honorum. He will supervise the state treasury and conduct audits for the Republic.
The Chieftans have declared this man as one of them. Who knows, maybe one day he will be the strongest of them all?
Nearing the top of the political pyramid can be both rewarding and precarious. This man has been promoted ever higher.
More and more power garners fame and fortune, but also adds responsibility and risk. This man has gained a higher office.
This man has been elected aedile. Although he has the seemingly benign task of overseeing construction and festivals, putting on a great show is a sure way toward political advancement.
A most august and respected man of Roma, this man has become Censor. Other men make way for this man's pre-eminent dignitas and gravitas

The pinnacle of the cursus honorum, this man has brought honor and fame to his family. The Consulship is only held by 2 men since the founding. He will go down as a scion of his family for generations to come.

This man has reached a pivotal point in his career and was elected one of the few Praetors for the year. His duties are varied and important to the Republic; his chances for advancement have just begun.
After serving as Consul, this man has had his command extended by the Senate. Glory to Roma!
After serving as Praetor for his allotted year, this man has had his duties extended by the senate.

After continued interaction between the Scythians and Sarmatians, they have slowly begun to adopt each other’s customs and techniques. Because of their cultural intermixing, expect to see new strategies from the Scythians and Sarmatians.

As a local tribe, the Getae rely on many similar tactics as other Dacian factions. However, if we ever end up facing the forces of extremely foreign and new peoples like the Nomadic cultures or Roma, our society will adapt as it did historically. Our progress as a culture relies on the crucible of battle and learning from those cultures adept at warfare - especially Roma.

Once a great satrapy and a kingdom in its own right, Atropatkan's nobles dream of the days of former glory. Re-uniting the lands of the old Persian empire and pushing back its many foes will help advance the culture and military expertise of our stalwart people.
To achieve the Indo-Saka reform, hold the provinces of Baktria and Transoxiana for 20 turns.

While the tribes that encompass the Royal Scythian nobility have begun to adopt some Hellenic practices and employ some of those techniques in battle, their soul still remains on the ancient steppes. The migration of Sarmatians into Scythian lands has brought conflict as well as cultural insight. As the Scythians and Sarmatians interact, our people will learn new strategies from each other. More and varied units will be available after that point in our march to dominance.
Our people have always dreamt of uniting once disparate tribes from the various isles of Britain. Perhaps one day we will achieve this worthy goal and advance our people forward.

Now that Rome is no longer at war in Southern Italy, the uneasy peace between Carthage and Rome has become frayed. Should Rome seek to assert her influence into Sicily, Carthage will take that as a sign of war.

Divide et Impera now includes a Population system. Before you  get to conquering, take a moment to read how these new mechanics work and  what new challenges lie ahead of you.\\n\\nPopulation is region based and represents the fighting age male population divided into 4 classes: Nobles, Warriors, Commoners, and Foreigners (with differing classifications for cultures). All units will require population to recruit, the population class needed will be dependent on the units that you recruit. Replenishment and garrisons also consume population.\\n\\nGrowth: Population will increase or decrease based on a number of factors including buildings, wars, settlement type, taxation and more. The tooltip shows an approximate growth and not an exact growth.\\n\\nImmigration: People will move to and from neighbouring regions within your empire. These movements are largely based on the ratio of each population class as well as a number of other factors. You can see how many people migrated to and from each region by hovering over the 3 people icon next to the regional population number.\\n\\nEconomics: The size and ratio of the different population classes will also have economic and public order impacts on your settlements.\\n\\nConquest: When you capture a settlement, all the people of in that settlement will become foreigners and your citizen population will have to grow. If you have the majority culture in the province, only half will become foreigners.\\n\\nNavies: Navies take all their population both for recruitment and replenishment from the faction's capital.\\n\\nCredit for all of these features goes to the PoR 2 mod team.

The region is devastated, regional supplies are mostly gone. The Impact on the region and armies without supply lines is disastrous. However, local supplies will regenerate over time if no military forces are located in the region.
This fleet is running low on supplies. It might be for the best to resupply in a nearby port.

After continued interaction between the Scythians and Sarmatians, your tribes have slowly begun to adopt their customs and techniques. Because of this cultural intermixing, you can now employ new strategies and units to help further your expansion and growth.

The British people have finally been united under one rule and the isles stand as a testament to a great culture. Various tribes from all British peoples come together to contribute to a burgeoning nation.

The Carthaginians have reached a new military reform.\\n\\nAs the enemies of Carthage develop better tactics and equipment, they must also change with the times. Carthaginian and Libyan soldiers are now equipped with better armour and specialized training in order to combat the improvements of their enemies and the inevitable changing ways of war.\\n\\nNew worldwide reforms are an ample military advancement for a culture. This advancement doesn't benefit only the interested factions, but has global repercussions: new auxiliary options will open up for all factions worldwide and new mercenaries can be hired.

Carthage has reached a new military reform.\\n\\nAs the enemies of Carthage develop better tactics and equipment, they must also change with the times. Carthaginian and Libyan soldiers are now equipped with better armour and specialized training in order to combat the improvements of their enemies and the inevitable changing ways of war.\\n\\nAnother player faction has reached a high enough Imperium Level to advance to a new reform. Differently from worldwide reforms this benefits exclusively that faction so beware of this, but you can also use the time required to adapt to the new reform as a good moment to start an attack if the faction is your enemy.

As the enemies of Carthage develop better tactics and equipment, we must also change with the times. Carthaginian and Libyan soldiers are now equipped with better armour and specialized training in order to combat the improvements of our enemies and the inevitable changing ways of war.\\n\\nNew reforms usually open up new equipment and new ways of combat, not necessarily stronger overall but usually more efficient for the time and for the challenges presented ahead. Some reforms can require a period of adaptation to the new tactics. Study well the differences in your troops because this can mean the difference between victory and defeat.

The Parni used to be insignificant tribe, just part of the Dahae Confederacy. Now, they have issued a challenge to Greek kings and satrapal puppets. They have taken Parthia and other lands and made them their own. Rather than keeping to their Nomadic life, they have embraced local customs and people. While Parthians haven't changed their ways of waging war after conquering lands to the South, they have adopted parts of local clothing, armours and even religion.\\nDuring the Parthian Kingdom, cavalry became even heavier and horse archers deadlier. Infantry was still only there to support cavalry but the increase in available manpower of conquered lands and their traditions provided more reliable foot soldiers.

From Syria to India, all lands and trade routes are under Parthian government. A vast Empire reigns supreme in the East and armies swell in numbers and grandeur.\\n This final reform represents the peak of the Parthian Empire and there are no enemies that they are unable to meet in the field. Nobles amass large estates and wealth, which allows them to cover their mounts as well as themselves fully in advanced armor. Nisean horses are carrying deadly cataphracts into combat and skilled horse archers are available in large numbers, from both freemen and nobles. Having experience with many sieges, infantry became more important and, while not as elite as for many other nations, it is now able to be very competent on the field.

We have reached a new military reform.\\n\\nAs the battles against our enemies have taught our people, an army must be willing to adapt and change with the times. After facing new types of warfare on the battlefield, the Getae have adapted their tactics and manner of warfare. We have begun to fight in more professional, heavily armoured and disciplined formations, laying the foundations of a new Dacian army.\\n\\nNew worldwide reforms are an ample military advancement for a culture and this is no exception. The Dacian kingdom sets its past glories up as an example for future conquest.\\n\\nThe Dacians and Getae were originally many different independent tribes, who later coalesced into a single confederacy and, in the case of the Dacians, later still, a centrally controlled kingdom. The precise stages of their expansion are still a complete mystery. The Dacians and Getae fought much against the Thracians, and captured the Hellenistic diadoch Lysimachus in 312 BC. In 82 BC, King Burebista ascended to the throne, and under his rule the Kingdom of Dacia reached its maximum extent. In 72 BC, Roman general Marcus Terentius Varro Lucullus marched against the Getae, because they were allied to Mithradates VI of Pontus, but he had only limited success. Ten years later, an alliance of Getae, Dacians, Scyths, Bastarnae and Greek colonists in Thrace defeated Gaius Antonius Hybrida (the same Hybrida who had shared a consulship with Cicero).\\n\\nThis victory allowed Burebista to expand his power up and down the Black Sea coast, and this in turn gave him the resources to complete his conquest of the Hallstatt-culture Celtic-speaking inhabitants of Pannonia. Burebista came to be considered such a threat to Roman-controlled Macedonia and Illyria that, in 44 BC, Julius Caesar was planning to march against him. However, he was prevented by his assassination. Burebista, coincidentally, was killed in the same year.

Ten years later, an alliance of Getae, Dacians, Scyths, Bastarnae and Greek colonists in Thrace defeated Gaius Antonius Hybrida (the same Hybrida who had shared a consulship with Cicero).\\n\\nThis victory allowed Burebista to expand his power up and down the Black Sea coast, and this in turn gave him the resources to complete his conquest of the Hallstatt-culture Celtic-speaking inhabitants of Pannonia. Burebista came to be considered such a threat to Roman-controlled Macedonia and Illyria that, in 44 BC, Julius Caesar was planning to march against him. However, he was prevented by his assassination. Burebista, coincidentally, was killed in the same year.

The Romans have reached a new military reform.\\n\\n The Republic is now just a part of Roman history, but a new era has emerged out of its bloody death throes. The countless civil wars have come to an end, and the Princeps is no longer the first man of the senate, but ruler of the entire empire, like a monarch of the East. The size and organisation of the Roman army has adapted to the needs of the new era, and the legions have become a fully professional standing army, augmented by auxiliaries from every corner of their vast empire. The emperor is now the highest commander (imperator) of the military, and the legions swear their oaths of loyalty only to him. The symbol of the legions has become the eagle (aquila) of Jupiter, and the Praetorian Guards have been converted from battlefield bodyguards to the emperors personal guard. In time these new imperial bodyguards will wield more power than anyone could imagine.\\n\\nNew worldwide reforms are an ample military advancement for a culture. This advancement doesn't benefit only the interested factions, but has global repercussions: new auxiliary options will open up for all factions worldwide and new mercenaries can be hired.

The Iberians reached a new military reform.\\n\\nImprovements in iron technology and warfare tactics have brought changes to the Iberian forces. Like their Greek and Roman rivals, highly disciplined Iberian soldiers are now outfitted with heavier armour. These new professional warriors use improved tactics and equipment to defeat their foes.\\n\\nNew worldwide reforms are an ample military advancement for a culture. This advancement doesn't benefit only the interested factions, but has global repercussions: new auxiliary options will open up for all factions worldwide and new mercenaries can be hired.

The Romans have reached a new military reform.\\n\\nThe massive changes caused by the enormous expansion of the Res Publica have brought Roman society to a tipping point. The number of small farmers and the potential draftees of the army is in a steady decline while the slaves working the latifundia estates spread over Italy. Every new year, it is difficult to recruit the necessary troops to defend their interests against the countless enemies of Roma. The number of men who meet the property requirements for military service is falling continuously. Even in the field, Rome's mighty legions have suffered some setbacks. But now one of their best generals has recognised the problems, and has begun a series of reforms to solve them and to improve the overall performance of the army. The old manipular distinctions are gone, and all legionaries carry standardised equipment provided by the state. The capite censi, property-less citizens, are now recruited into the legions. However, without property requirements for service, new recruits come increasingly from the lower classes of society, eager to earn wealth and glory in the legions. The class of professional soldiers is born.\\n\\nNew worldwide reforms are an ample military advancement for a culture. This advancement doesn't benefit only the interested factions, but has global repercussions: new auxiliary options will open up for all factions worldwide and new mercenaries can be hired.

Atropatkan has reached a new military reform.\\n\\nThe lands of Iran were a field of many wars fought between various nations. The once formidable Persian empire crumbled and its noble houses diminished. Greeks, Parthians and even Romans fought over these lands for centuries without any side being able to deal a fatal blow. After long and bloody dynastic struggles between noble houses, a victor emerged and united everyone under one banner. Shahanshah, King of Kings, is now again leading the armies of Eranshahr. Long wars and hard fought battles have created a force to be reckoned with and the development of cavalry equipment has become much more advanced with heavier horse armour and early saddles. Armies are resembling a feudal system of nobles levied for war, with instructions of what types of weapons and armours are required of them.

Atropatkan has reached a new military reform.\\n\\nAs time progressed so did the fighting ways of the Iranian people. Due to Nomadic influence, cavalry equipment became much heavier while deadly kontus lances became the main shock weapons. Mounted javeliners of the late Achaemenids went out of fashion and bows once again were a favored tool of war for Median riders. The influx of Greeks all over the old empire allowed it to adapt recent changes in warfare with their pikes and thureos shields.

The Celts have reached a new military reform.\\n\\nDramatic political and military events have shaken the Celtic world; the Celts have come into contact with many different cultures as their territories expanded, each bringing their own knowledge of tactics and equipment. As a result of increased contact with the heavily armed forces of the Mediterranean peoples, wealthier elements of Celtic society are now equipping themselves as heavy infantry and heavy cavalry. The warrior class has greatly expanded, with leaders who have expressed their willingness to pay for the equipment of their soldiers in order to defend the expanding territory from the myriad foreign threats outside Celtic domains. Improvements in training and iron technology give rise to a more professional soldier who sees warfare as his primary duty. These soldiers are tactically proficient and disciplined in formation. This change has opened new possibilities for the organisation, composition and equipment of the Celtic army.\\n\\nNew worldwide reforms are an ample military advancement for a culture. This advancement doesn't benefit only the interested factions, but has global repercussions: new auxiliary options will open up for all factions worldwide and new mercenaries can be hired.

The Germans have reached a new military reform.\\n\\nA combination of external threats and internal disputes have caused the development of more professional armies among the Germanic tribes. The Romans have reached a new military reform.\\n\\nThe wars of the past decades and continual bouts with Italian forces have somehow proven fruitful, as the influence of Roma grows beyond its humble Italian origins. Their consuls have perfected manipular tactics, the old hoplite phalanx replaced by three lines of maniples, sorted by age and experience instead of class and wealth: the young hastati, backed up by older, more heavily armed principes, followed by the veteran triarii in reserve. Their military has also adopted some of the armaments and armour of their enemies, their utility proven by the mercenaries of Poenii and the warriors of the Celtae. Roman soldiers now carry pila (javelins), and hastati and principes both have replaced their hastae (spears) with the deadly shortsword called the gladius. The older triarii, reluctant to change, still fight as a hoplite phalanx. Accensi and leves are no longer used, while velites become the chosen skirmisher unit.

As we migrate to remote areas and our warriors learn from each native tribe, we have made use of the military knowledge of the foreign peoples we have encountered. We find that these strangers are often well-organised and their soldiers unusual, but they are in no way useless, fighting on foot, heavily armoured and in disciplined formations. The experience of our warriors and the recruitment of subjugated people can now change the way in which we make war. It is now possible to lay the foundations of a new Indo-Saka army.\\n\\nNew reforms usually open up new equipment and new ways of combat, not necessarily stronger overall but usually more efficient for the time and for the challenges presented ahead. The Scythians have reached a new military reform.\\n\\nAs they migrate to remote areas and their warriors learn from each native tribe, the Scythians have made use of the military knowledge of the foreign peoples they have encountered. They have begun to fight on foot, heavily armoured and in disciplined formations, laying the foundations of a new Indo-Saka army.\\n\\nNew worldwide reforms are an ample military advancement for a culture. This advancement doesn't benefit only the interested factions, but has global repercussions: new auxiliary options will open up for all factions worldwide and new mercenaries can be hired.

The Greeks have reached a new military reform.\\n\\nThorax Reforms\\nAs the need for better equipped, more disciplined and highly trained troops becomes apparent, the wide-scale use of Thorax plated armour emerges. Along with this better armour, some soldiers are trained to fight in the Roman style, using javelins and a legionary organisation. While still using the thureos, the improved armour and training will produce elite Hellenic warriors ready to take on any task on the battlefield.\\n\\nNabis Spartan Reforms\\nThe Spartan state had fallen into a lull until Nabis, the regent of adolescent king Pelops, would overthrow and kill the last legitimate monarch and proclaim himself the king (and tyrant by others). He exiled the nobles and divided the land to others. Many slaves were granted citizenship but the institution of helotry would remain. Nabis reconquered some of the lost lands and developed a Spartan fleet with help from the Cretans, who were his allies. Later on, due to Roman influence and an unstable situation in Greece, plus constantly changing allies, Nabis' territory was reduced to just city of Sparta. At this point, he formed an alliance with Antiochus III but in the end, he got killed by Aetolians, who had been his allies.

The Greeks reached a new military reform.\\n\\nThureos Reforms\\nThe development of the thureos has altered unit equipment and training. A large oval shield made of leather-covered wood and braced by a spined boss, the thureos was first adopted from the Galatians by the Illyrians and Thracians. Some say it resembles the Oscan scutum brought back after Pyrrhus' Italian campaign. However it made its way into Greek hands, it is now used by the thureophoroi, who have become tactically flexible troops in the Greek army.\\n\\nCleomenes Spartan Reforms\\nAfter losing many wars, citizens and their former spirit, Sparta during the early 3rd century BC was just a shadow of former glory. They were still a force to be reckoned with, but only slightly above other Greek states until king Cleomenes III would start his reforms. He increased the pool of Spartan citizens by bringing back banished Spartans and giving the land to mercenaries. He also rearmed part of the Spartan army in Macedonian style but only by forming own pike units, cavalry was still made from Thessalian mercenaries. While very successful at first, Cleomenes was defeated badly by Antigonus III Dioson at Sellasia in 222 BC. This again pushed Sparta back.

He also rearmed part of the Spartan army in Macedonian style but only by forming own pike units, cavalry was still made from Thessalian mercenaries. While very successful at first, Cleomenes was defeated badly by Antigonus III Dioson at Sellasia in 222 BC. This again pushed Sparta back.
Your army's provisions have been stolen by an enemy spy. Many troops demoralized and your army is slowed.
Your spy infiltrated the enemy camp and has stolen the provisions. Their loss is your gain.
War in Italy. The Senate of Rome enlists Legions to defend the Republic. As long as Rome and the city are not taken by force, Rome will fill the ranks of the army to repel any aggressor.
War in Italy. The Senate of Rome enlists Legions to defend the Republic. As long as Rome and the city are not taken by force, Rome will fill the ranks of the army to repel any aggressor.
Your newly acquired region has special attributes that make it different from other regions. These include things like trade, fertility and public order.
Carthage is gaining the upper hand in Sicily. On its way to complete control over the island of Sicily, Carthage has angered Roman interests in the area.
The city of Syrakousai has fallen to the Carthaginians. With the threat of a Punic Sicily on its doorstep, Roma now views their former allies in Carthage as enemies. War has begun.

With Roman armies marching into Sicily, the relationship between Rome and Carthage has deteriorated into open warfare. Carthage will respond by quickly mustering a force of their own to repel the Roman expedition.

Carthaginian forces were decisively beaten in Lilybaion. The Carthaginians have lost their foothold on the island, and reorganize their defense near Carthage's coastline, only a small journey away from Lilybaion.
Supplies are plentiful and easy to come by.
Armies have taken a terrible toll on the region. Supplies are low.
Supplies are still manageable within the region.

Every army (more than a single general) on the campaign map now requires supplies. Regions provide supplies based on their fertility rating found under province effects. You can check each region's supplies by mousing over the supply icon at the top right of the province panel.

Carthage and Syracuse are at or on the brink of war, in an attempt to gain control over the island of Sicily. A small Carthaginian expeditionary force has already set foot on the island. This will rouse the intense interest of the Roman Republic.

Instead of changing government types, the Senones can request their [[col:green]]druids[[/col]] to offer their warriors divine guidance.\\n\\nHaving arrived in Italy only a few years ago, the Senones are eager to build their fortune and claim new lands for their people. Constantly on the march forward, their armies have [[col:green]]increased movement range[[/col]] but their settlements [[col:red]]grow slower[[/col]]. do not [[col:red]]peacefully occupy settlements[[/col]], only [[col:yellow]]loot, raze[[/col]] or [[col:yellow]]sack[[/col]] them!

Instead of changing government types, Syracuse can attempt to dispatch a [[col:green]]colonising expedition[[/col]] to settle distant lands. A successfully-established colony provides benefits for the mother city.\\n\\nSyracuse can also [[col:green]]hire unique mercenaries[[/col]] unavailable to other factions, which are of great help during their constant conflict against mighty Carthage.\\n\\nHowever, this dependecy on Mercenaries comes at the expense of the regular military, causing [[col:red]]higher non-Mercenary recruitment costs[[/col]].

Instead of changing government types, the Samnites can initiate the [[col:green]]Ver Sacrum[[/col]] rite, which spawns an army of young men, eager to find a new home of their own, near their capital.\\n\\nThe Samnites are intelligent warriors, able to use every kind of [[col:green]]terrain[[/col]] to their troops’ advantage when their armies are in [[col:green]]normal stance[[/col]].

Instead of changing government types, the Insubres’ King can request the [[col:green]]druids[[/col]] to assist with the control of commoners or noblemen.\\n\\nSettled around strategic Alpine passages for centuries, the Insubres have had a degree of control over the flow of people and valuable goods such as [[col:green]]amber[[/col]] coming into into Italy from the north. \\n\\nThis has made them sly negotiators who can [[col:green]]create client states[[/col]], but being comfortable in their local dominance, their armies have [[col:red]]reduced campaign movement range.[[/col]]

Instead of changing government types, the Veneti High King can instruct the royal stablemasters to [[col:green]]either breed warhorses[[/col]] or workhorses[[/col]] for military or civil endeavours, respectively.\\n\\nThe very strongest warhorses are selected for the military, able to support heavily armed and armored riders, giving the Veneti [[col:green]]the best cavalry in Italy[[/col]].\\n\\nVenetic culture is unique, originating in the Celto-Illyrian Pannonia and influenced by Etruscans and other Italian peoples. Estranged and frequently embroiled in conflict with their cousins, the Veneti suffer a [[col:red]]major diplomatic penalty[[/col]] with Celtic and Illyrian factions.

Instead of changing government types, Tarchuna can [[col:green]]hold a summit[[/col]] at the revered shrine of Fanum Voltumnae, leading to various factionwide effects.\\n\\nDue to their highly elitistic society, Tarchuna suffers higher base [[col:red]]Empire Maintenance[[/col]] and lower base [[col:red]]Public Order[[/col]] than other factions.\\n\\nThe Etruscans of marvellous Tarchuna must be cautious, for if they [[col:red]]lose their capital city[[/col]], they will fall into disunity and eventual oblivion, thus [[col:red]]losing them the game[[/col]].

Instead of changing government types, the Iolei can appease the gods and the people alike by holding a [[col:green]]ritual tournament[[/col]], with positive effects on every sphere of life. As one of the most influential and populous Nuragic tribes, the Ioleans have additional [[col:green]]recruitment capacity[[/col]] in their capital. \\n\\nInsular, devout, and fiercly protective of their way of life and sacred lands, the Ioleans are wary of foreigners, causing [[col:red]]penalty to public order from foreign cultures[[/col]] in their territories.\\n\\nThe Nuragic peoples are masters of [[col:green]]unconventional warfare[[/col]] such as guerilla warfare and fighting at night.

Instead of changing government types, Taras has access to the services provided by its [[col:green]]court philosophers[[/col]]. They can be assigned to conduct [[col:green]]research[[/col]] which, depending on the wise men’s whims, may unlock a technology.\\n\\nPhilosophers can also be tasked with writing a[[col:green]] treatise[[/col]] on a topic of their choosing. Once completed, a [[col:green]]treatise[[/col]] provides a variety of benefits. \\n\\nHowever, when it comes to putting theory into practice for civic development, the learned will often dispute, leading to [[col:red]]longer building construction times[[/col]].

Instead of changing government types, Rome has access to [[col:green]]elections[[/col]] that enable the appointment of [[col:green]]consuls[[/col]] or a [[col:green]]dictator[[/col]] for a limited amount of turns. Outside of these elections, Rome's political rank system promotes generals who have won battles and are of a certain age rather than through the Secure Promotion political action.\\n\\nThe city of Rome itself, although relatively young, is very well defended and capture of the fortified [[col:green]]Capitoline Hill[[/col]] requires an enemy to defeat the Romans in [[col:green]]two separate battles[[/col]] in order to capture the city.

Your impressive growth has sparked concern amongst other factions, and they mean to stop you! A coalition of enemies have formed against you. Open your diplomacy screen to see who foolishly took up arms! Hint: Achieving peace with HALF of the coalition will DISBAND IT!
Rival nations have banded together in pursuit of a common enemy! They aim to change the balance of powwer in the region..
You have beaten back the forces that combined to keep you at bay!
Your coalition status has changed.
